label: Brasília
sub: Brazil — founding BRICS member (2009); 2025 BRICS Chair

label: Moscow
sub: Russia — founding BRICS member (2009); hosted 2020 (virtual) and 2024 Kazan summits

label: New Delhi
sub: India — founding BRICS member (2009); 2021 and 2026 BRICS Chair

label: Beijing
sub: China — founding BRICS member (2009); largest economy in bloc by PPP; NDB headquarters in Shanghai

label: Johannesburg
sub: South Africa — joined Dec 2010 (effective 2011); hosted 2013 and 2023 expansion summits

label: Cairo
sub: Egypt — joined BRICS Jan 1, 2024; brings Suez Canal and regional Arab influence

label: Addis Ababa
sub: Ethiopia — joined BRICS Jan 1, 2024; sub-Saharan Africa's second-largest economy

label: Tehran
sub: Iran — joined BRICS Jan 1, 2024 under international sanctions; most geopolitically contentious new member

label: Abu Dhabi
sub: UAE — joined BRICS Jan 1, 2024; major global trade and financial hub bridging East and West

label: Jakarta
sub: Indonesia — joined BRICS Jan 7, 2025; first Southeast Asian member; world's 4th-largest population

label: Yekaterinburg
sub: 1st BRIC Summit — June 16, 2009; inaugural meeting of Brazil, Russia, India, China

label: Sanya
sub: 3rd BRICS Summit — April 2011; first summit including South Africa; BRICS name adopted

label: Fortaleza
sub: 6th BRICS Summit — July 2014; New Development Bank and CRA founded

label: Ufa
sub: 7th BRICS Summit — July 2015; NDB formally opened; Russia chaired amid post-Crimea sanctions

label: Xiamen
sub: 9th BRICS Summit — Sep 2017; 'BRICS Plus' concept launched; first RMB-denominated NDB bond

label: Kazan
sub: 16th BRICS Summit — Oct 2024; 13 partner countries admitted; largest diplomatic event in Russia since 2022

label: Rio de Janeiro
sub: 17th BRICS Summit — July 6-7, 2025; AI governance declaration and Global South reform agenda

label: NDB Shanghai
sub: New Development Bank HQ — opened Jul 2015; $35.7B+ approved; ~28% local currency lending

label: Riyadh
sub: Saudi Arabia — invited to join BRICS Aug 2023; froze membership bid late 2024; hedging between BRICS and US ties

label: Ankara
sub: Turkey — admitted as BRICS Partner Country at Kazan Oct 2024; NATO member seeking multi-vector diplomacy
